name: First Interstate BancSystem Foundation

name: GARDNER FOUNDATION

name: GARY E MILGARD FAMILY FOUNDATION - MARK
reasoning: Website FAQ explicitly states 'No, we only award to nonprofit 501(c)3 organizations' in response to 'We are not classified as a 501(c)3' — VetsBoats as a private foundation (990-PF filer) would not qualify. name: GEORGE S AND DOLORES DORE ECCLES FOUNDATION
reasoning: Mission explicitly states the foundation is 'dedicated to serving the people of the State of Utah' — all five focus areas (Arts/Culture, Community, Education, Health/Wellness, Preservation/Conservation) are Utah-only. name: GREEN BAY PACKERS FOUNDATION
reasoning: NFL team foundation — scraped website is the Packers' main team site (packers.com), not the foundation's grantmaking page. name: GUPTA FAMILY FOUNDATION
reasoning: Mission is focused on helping disadvantaged individuals become self-reliant through small organizations worldwide; they require board members to personally conduct site visits before funding, and their process requires 501(c)(3) status is implied. No evidence they fund private foundations or adaptive recreation programs.

name: HAMILTON FAMILY FOUNDATION
reasoning: Strictly funds K-12 educational programs in Philadelphia PA, Palm Beach County FL, and Aquidneck Island RI — no California, no veterans, no adaptive recreation. Zero mission overlap with VetsBoats.

name: HANSEN FAMILY FOUNDATION
reasoning: FAQ explicitly states 'all grant recipients must be a charitable organization recognized by the US IRS as a 501(c)3' — private foundations are ineligible. However, the chairman is a US Naval Academy graduate and retired Navy officer (23 years), and their focus areas include Humanitarian Support, with grants up to $50K. Mission overlap is moderate given the naval/military leadership, but the explicit 501(c)(3) requirement disqualifies VetsBoats as a private foundation.

name: HE HILL FOUNDATION INC
reasoning: Primary purpose is affordable housing for working families in Western Palm Beach, Hendry, and Glades Counties of Florida. While they have 33 veteran grants (mostly Wreaths Across America, wounded warrior events in FL), these are all Florida-based and the foundation's core mission is Florida housing — not adaptive recreation, not California.

name: HEALTHSPARK FOUNDATION
reasoning: Strictly serves Montgomery County, Pennsylvania — website states 'it is very unlikely for us to fund organizations outside of the Oklahoma City core' (actually Montgomery County PA). Focused on community power, housing justice, and equity in PA. No mission overlap with adaptive sailing for veterans in SF Bay Area.

name: HEES FAMILY FOUNDATION
reasoning: Very small foundation ($33.5K annual giving) in Tennessee, founded in memory of a Marine Corps veteran, now supporting local scholarships and small nonprofits. One veteran grant of $1,000 in 2022 was incidental. No application process found, too small to be viable, and no California/adaptive recreation focus.

name: HEFFERNAN GROUP FOUNDATION
reasoning: California-based insurance company foundation (Walnut Creek CA) focused on education, scholarships, and community events tied to their employees. One veteran grant ($5K in 2020) was a small incidental donation to a military families charity. No evidence of grant application process for external nonprofits, and as a corporate foundation tied to employee giving, external nonprofit applications are not the model.

name: Harry S & Isabel C Cameron Foundation
reasoning: Explicitly restricted to organizations 'located inside the Archdiocese of Galveston-Houston' (specific SE Texas counties only). Has Catholic affiliation preference. While they have 6 veteran grants, all are Texas-based. Geographic restriction to Houston/SE Texas makes VetsBoats in SF Bay Area ineligible.

name: Helen V Brach Foundation

Based on grant history, they fund in Illinois and nationally with 9 veteran grants ($52.5K), but all focus on veteran homelessness, military families, and Catholic-affiliated programs. name: Hilda & Preston Davis Foundation
reasoning: Focuses on eating disorders, education for the underprivileged, and youth development in the northeastern US (southern New England, Middle Atlantic states) and California. Six veteran grants are entirely for veteran housing (Project HOME PA) and Wounded Warrior Project — no adaptive recreation or sailing connection. No application process is visible on their site.

reasoning: Private foundation serving Greater Houston only — four focus areas are Public Education, Civic Engagement, Arts/Culture, and Greenspaces. Veteran grants in their history relate to college/career/military readiness in Houston school districts and voter access. No mission overlap with adaptive sailing for veterans in SF Bay Area.

name: INASMUCH FOUNDATION

name: INTERNATIONAL PAPER COMPANY FOUNDATION
reasoning: Corporate foundation tied to International Paper's employee communities; signature causes are Education, Hunger, Disaster Relief, and Health/Wellness. name: JA & KATHRYN ALBERTSON FOUNDATION INC
reasoning: Website FAQ explicitly states 'The Foundation does not accept unsolicited grant requests.' Idaho-only foundation focused on innovative learning, recreation, and 'life after the military' (specifically in Idaho). name: JUBITZ FAMILY FOUNDATION
reasoning: Oregon-based foundation focused on peace (specifically anti-militarism and War Prevention Initiative), environmental peacebuilding, and Mount Hood conservation. Their one 'veteran' grant was $10K to American University for 'Strategizing to Dismantle the Military Industrial Complex' — an anti-militarism grant, not a veteran services grant. Mission is fundamentally opposed to VetsBoats' veteran-serving, sailing-based model.

name: Jacksonville Jaguars Foundation Inc
reasoning: NFL team foundation with strong veteran grant history (47 grants, $882K) — but all concentrated in Jacksonville/Northeast Florida area. Scraped site is the main Jaguars team website with no foundation application information. As a sports team local foundation, all grantmaking is Jacksonville-centric; no path for a CA private foundation.

name: Jacob and Terese Hershey Foundation
reasoning: Texas-only focus (Houston and central Texas); website explicitly states 'outside Texas by invitation only.'
